page: Amazon Web Services i7i.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processors. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture instance features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, which provides 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The server includes 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 169,956.68 and a Passmark CPU score of 131,162.12. The DDR5 memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth, reaching 569.6 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Database performance is highly competitive, with Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations both ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Additionally, the CPU supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which contribute to strong performance in LLM inference tasks, particularly for prompt processing.

Given its massive local NVMe SSD capacity and high-density memory configuration, the i7i.48xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive, storage-heavy workloads. The combination of 45 TB of local storage and top-tier database transaction performance makes this instance highly suitable for large-scale relational databases, NoSQL systems, and real-time data analytics. It is also well-suited for high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and memory-bound enterprise applications that require high-speed local data access without the latency of network-attached storage.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.48xlarge is a storage-optimized server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 192 vCPUs from dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processors, 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. With a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, this instance is engineered for high-throughput data access. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 205.98 million operations per second. The CPU includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enhancing vector and matrix calculations. This server is highly efficient for workloads requiring massive local storage and high memory density, making it an optimal fit for large-scale databases, data analytics, and high-throughput enterprise applications.

tagline: A high-density storage and memory-optimized server featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity and top-tier multi-core processing performance.

1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz
45,000 GB of high-performance local NVMe SSD storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier database, Redis, and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Optimized for large-scale databases, data analytics, and enterprise workloads
